decision: API-bound JSON Schema is a runtime grammar gate only. Safe migration additionally requires application-owned raw parse, business identity, semantic safety, protected-value, cancellation, persistence, and product-behavior gates.

analysis_surfaces: host compatibility request and payload construction
host block schema, processing, retry, fallback, ordering, persistence, cancellation, and tests
host image schema, parsing, cancellation, result construction, and tests
LabKit structured validation and vision closure evidence

api_bound_schema.interpretation: Block and image paths use strict closed JSON Schema through the compatibility payload; plain text chunk cleanup remains unstructured.

transport_retry.risk: Transport attempts and the structural retry can multiply without one logical-request call ceiling.

vision_post_generation.risk: Schema defects can become apparently usable results, while structural validity remains independent from OCR and grounding quality.

fail_closed_gates: Request generation is current and not cancelled.
Transport completed with final text and safe finish/usage state.
Raw JSON parses without silent repair, unwrap, or defaults.
A local full JSON Schema validator accepts the closed payload.
Expected count, ID sequence, uniqueness, order, and non-empty string types match exactly.
Only authoritative target units are changed or summarized.
Application-derived protected values and placeholders are unchanged.
Task-specific semantic safety passes; image dimensions remain separate.
Cancellation and request generation are rechecked immediately before commit.
Output, control state, and validator summary commit atomically and pass read-back.

structural.retry_change: Keep source and semantic instruction fixed; add one terse correction naming only the failed structural gate.

retry_policy.global_rule: Attempt zero and retry one are immutable; define one total-call ceiling so transport and structural retries cannot multiply invisibly.

fallback_policy.structural_exhaustion: Return all original target blocks unchanged in application order and mark fallback_original.
fallback_policy.semantic_or_safety_failure: Skip structural retry and return original targets unchanged.
fallback_policy.summary_or_image_without_safe_original_e: Remain failed or unavailable; do not publish a structurally valid guess.

persistence_contract.authoritative_order: original expected sequence, not numeric sorting unless domain-guaranteed equivalent
persistence_contract.commit_fence: Only the current non-cancelled request generation may commit.
persistence_contract.fallback_if_no_transaction: Use explicit pending/committed state plus deterministic recovery and read-back.

image_semantic_gate.closed_world: Use exact or explicitly tolerance-scored references where available.
image_semantic_gate.open_world: Use an independently reviewed grounding rubric with abstain or review_required outcomes.
image_semantic_gate.non_claim: The bounded 36/36 raw/schema result does not establish production semantic admission.

rollout.requirement: Exercise every taxonomy branch, attempt ledger, cancellation fence, and persistence failure with fake clients.
Validate privacy-safe categories only; never replace or persist user-visible output.
Compare accepted candidates with unchanged source and manual review by task and source position.
One task contract at a time with original-source fallback, kill switch, call/latency ceiling, and automatic rollback.
Task-specific semantic and persistence thresholds pass; no cross-task inference.

actions: Disable new local structured requests by contract-specific kill switch.
Advance request generation so in-flight completions are inert.
Continue with original application-owned source and timestamps.
Retain traceability from committed output to accepted attempt, schema, and validator versions.

missing_tests: Duplicate, extra, reordered, wrong-type, empty-text, and bare-array block responses fail closed.
Structural retry is distinct from transport retry, has a total-call ceiling, and preserves both attempts.
Fallback preserves exact original text, ID order, timestamps, degraded state, persistence read-back, and export.
Cancellation before request, during retry/backoff, in flight, after validation, and during commit prevents late persistence.
Aggregate and per-block writes have transaction, failure-injection, and recovery coverage.
Protected names, numbers, dates, URLs, commands, placeholders, and cross-boundary leakage have deterministic fixtures.
Usage-at-cap and runaway repetition fail despite a normal stop reason.
Image parsing rejects repaired/defaulted schema failures and semantic tests cover OCR, grounding, completeness, and unsupported claims.
Shadow/canary kill switch, stale generation, rollback, schema version, and validator version have integration coverage.
Metrics expose attempt, fallback, validator, and persistence outcomes without raw content.
evidence_gaps: No retained end-to-end host run demonstrates structural retry, original fallback, persistence, read-back, and export together.
No cancellation race has been exercised against a real local structured request.
No valid binary image semantic-admission rubric is established.
No production threshold exists for protected-value or semantic acceptance.
No transaction or read-repair evidence exists for partial persistence failure.
Bounded model evidence does not establish broad model ranking, cross-task admission, or production concurrency.
